data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <bead id="ddx-c3efa754">
    <title>shared skill installer + ddx init rewire (cli/internal/skills)</title>
    <description>
Bead 1 of 4 children under epic ddx-3562c48c. Depends on bead 0 (ddx-2d9026d0) for spec.

Extract a shared skill installer used by both ddx init and ddx install. Real-files-only; no symlinks. Wire ddx init to it.

NEW FILE: cli/internal/skills/install.go
```go
package skills
import "io/fs"
type Options struct { Force bool }
func Install(src fs.FS, projectRoot string, opts Options) error
```

Behavior:
- Copies skills from src into <projectRoot>/.agents/skills/<name>/ and <projectRoot>/.claude/skills/<name>/.
- Real files only. NEVER creates symlinks.
- Path traversal defense: rejects '..', absolute paths, symlink-escape outside projectRoot — return non-nil error before any write.
- Force semantics: pre-existing SYMLINK destinations removed UNCONDITIONALLY before write. Pre-existing REAL-FILE destinations skipped per-skill (whole dir) when Force=false; overwritten when Force=true.
- Tarball broken-symlink recovery: when source's .agents/skills/<skill> is broken symlink, fall back to <source>/skills/<skill>.
- embed.FS satisfies fs.FS (used by init for bootstrap ddx skill); plugin path uses os.DirFS(<projectRoot>/.ddx/plugins/<plugin>) (covered in bead 2).
- .ddx/skills/ registration stays bootstrap-only — plugin skills do NOT get registered there.

WIRING:
- cli/cmd/init.go:256: replace registerProjectSkills call with skills.Install(skills.SkillFiles, workingDir, skills.Options{Force: opts.Force}).
- DELETE registerProjectSkills (cli/cmd/init.go:464+).
- DELETE registerSkills (cli/cmd/init.go:337) — the function that writes ~/.agents/skills/. Audit and remove all callers.

OUT OF SCOPE: registry installer, local install (--local flag), audit/uninstall, ddx install --global removal, .gitignore, docs. (Those are beads 2 and 4.)
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. cli/internal/skills/install.go exists with exported Install(fs.FS, projectRoot, Options) and type Options struct{Force bool}. 2. TestInstall_FromEmbedFS_CopiesBootstrapSkill exists and passes — asserts real files for bootstrap ddx skill, no symlinks. 3. TestInstall_BrokenTarballSymlinkRecovery exists and passes — broken source symlink falls back to <source>/skills/<skill>, destination is real file. 4. TestInstall_RejectsPathTraversal exists and passes — '..', absolute path, and symlink-escape inputs return errors before any write. 5. TestInstall_PreExistingSymlinkRemovedUnconditionally exists — Force=false still removes existing symlink dest. 6. TestInstall_ForceFalseSkipsRealDir / TestInstall_ForceTrueOverwritesRealDir cover Force semantics. 7. cli/cmd/init.go:256 calls skills.Install with skills.SkillFiles. 8. registerProjectSkills deleted. 9. registerSkills (init.go:337) deleted; no remaining callers. 10. cd cli && go build ./... passes. 11. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/skills/... passes (registry tests not yet updated — covered in bead 2).
    </acceptance>
    <labels>area:cli, area:install, kind:feature, spec:FEAT-015</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-3562c48c" spec-id="FEAT-015" base-rev="a146ad07d96c1e2261bb85aae12ecf17379dccd9" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260429T142618-064ee2d5"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <ref id="FEAT-015" path="docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-015-installation-architecture.md">Feature: DDx Installation Architecture</ref>
  </governing>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> sections above are the completion contract — every AC checkbox must be provably satisfied by a specific piece of code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## How to work

1. Read first. If the bead description names files, read them to see what is already there. If the bead references a spec, ADR, or API contract, read the relevant sections before writing code. Do not start editing until you understand what the bead wants changed and why.

2. Cross-reference your work against the acceptance criteria as you go. Before committing, walk through the AC one item at a time and identify the specific test name, file path, or function that satisfies each checkbox. If you cannot point to concrete evidence for an AC item, it is not done.

3. Run the project's verification commands before committing. Use whatever commands match the crate or package you touched — typically `cargo test` and `cargo clippy` for Rust, `bun test` or `npm test` for JS/TS, `go test` for Go. The bead description usually names the exact commands. **Do not commit red code.** If a test or lint fails, fix it first.

4. Commit once, when everything is green. Stage only the files you intentionally changed with `git add <specific-paths>` — never `git add -A`, because there may be unrelated WIP in this worktree. Use a conventional-commit-style subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. DDx will merge your commits back to the base branch.

5. If you cannot complete the bead in this pass, write your reasoning to `.ddx/executions/20260429T142618-064ee2d5/no_changes_rationale.txt` with: (a) what is done, (b) what is blocking, (c) what a follow-up attempt would need. Do NOT commit partial, exploratory, or red code hoping the reviewer will accept it — a well-justified no_changes is better than a bad commit. The bead will be re-queued for another attempt, potentially with a stronger model.

## The bead contract overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override any CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or project-level conservative defaults in this worktree. If the bead asks for new documentation, write it. If the bead adds a new module or crate, add it. Conservative rules (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) do not apply inside execute-bead — the bead IS the ask.

## Quality bar and the review step

After your commit merges, an automated review step may check your work against the acceptance criteria. If any AC item is unmet, the bead reopens and escalates to a higher-capability model, and the review findings are threaded into the next attempt's prompt as a `<review-findings>` section. **The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet the AC in this pass so the bead closes cleanly.**

If this prompt already contains a `<review-findings>` section, address every BLOCKING finding before claiming the work complete. Those findings are the precise list of what the previous attempt missed.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is already initialized. Running it corrupts the bead queue and project configuration.
- Do not modify files outside the scope the bead description names.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or any other project-instructions file unless the bead explicitly asks for it.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and you have verified every AC item, stop. Return control to the orchestrator. Do not continue to explore the repository, run extra tests, or generate follow-up notes — the bead is complete, and returning promptly is how execute-bead signals success.
  </instructions>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Bead complete. Commit `d78b62b8`:

- `cli/internal/skills/install.go` — exported `Install(fs.FS, projectRoot, Options)` + `Options{Force}`, writes real files only to `.agents/skills/` and `.claude/skills/`, rejects path traversal/absolute/symlink-escape before any write, removes pre-existing symlinks unconditionally, falls back to `<src>/skills/<name>` for broken-tarball-symlink recovery.
- `cli/internal/skills/install_test.go` — six tests covering all AC scenarios; all pass.
- `cli/cmd/init.go` — wired to `skills.Install`; deleted `registerSkills` (~/.agents writer) and `registerProjectSkills`; `.ddx/skills/` bootstrap retained via small private `registerBootstrapDDxSkills`.
- `cli/cmd/update.go` — `refreshShippedSkills` rewired to `skills.Install` + bootstrap helper.
- `cli/cmd/init_test.go` — deleted-function caller rewritten as e2e via `te.RunCommand("init")`.

`go build ./...` clean. Skills tests pass. Init/cleanup tests pass. The three lefthook-flagged failing test suites (`TestAgentCompare/QuorumDispatch...`, `TestBeadReviewCollapseGolden`, `TestAcceptance_US028-031/034`, `TestInstallationPerformance`) fail identically on baseline `a146ad07` — pre-existing and out of scope.
